Report-card table from a gradepath fit as a data frame

Source: R/accessors.R
as.data.frame.gp_fit.Rd

as.data.frame() method for gp_fit: returns the report-card rows – the underlying data frame of the gp_report_card slot (get_report_card(fit)$table) – with row names reset to the default sequence. Provided because as.data.frame() is the base generic an R user reaches for first (GP-DEC-13-A, Chapter 13 patterns; Chapter 14 ux). Read-only; recomputes nothing. Grade labels are integers in {1, ..., n} and carry no ranking-superiority statement of any kind.

Usage

# S3 method for class 'gp_fit'
as.data.frame(x, ...)

Arguments

x

A gp_fit object.

...

Unused; accepted for S3 method extensibility. No arguments are forwarded; supplying any has no effect.

Value

A data.frame: the report-card table (one row per unit), row names reset to the default integer sequence.

See also

get_report_card(), coef.gp_fit()

Other gradepath-accessors: coef.gp_fit(), get_control(), get_grades(), get_pairwise(), get_posterior(), get_prior(), get_report_card()

Examples

# Instant: the report-card rows of the bundled tiny fit as a data frame.
fit <- readRDS(system.file("extdata/examples/tiny_fit.rds", package = "gradepath"))
head(as.data.frame(fit))   # the report-card rows
#>       id  label grade sort_rank selected_lambda posterior_mean       lower
#> 1 firm03 firm03     1         1            0.25      0.1330361 0.004466502
#> 2 firm05 firm05     1         2            0.25      0.1547879 0.094098498
#> 3 firm10 firm10     1         3            0.25      0.1372338 0.024811477
#> 4 firm18 firm18     1         4            0.25      0.1166670 0.003301362
#> 5 firm24 firm24     1         5            0.25      0.1206680 0.004841016
#> 6 firm01 firm01     1         6            0.25      0.1323136 0.080808125
#>       upper estimate     se
#> 1 0.2014888   0.3431 0.2386
#> 2 0.1890643   0.4332 0.1309
#> 3 0.1749419   0.2895 0.1142
#> 4 0.1895925   0.1811 0.1902
#> 5 0.1782374   0.2017 0.1398
#> 6 0.1631853   0.2355 0.0838
